advertisers.usage_instructions: Read-only discovery on top of the CJ Advertiser Lookup REST API. Use these to find advertiser programs (merchants/brands) in the CJ Marketplace by category, keyword, name, or relationship slice. No browser session is required.

get.help: Get full detail for a single advertiser program.
get.usage_instructions: Fetch the full record for one advertiser by ID (commission terms, link types, actions, category, EPC, current relationship status). Pair with `--properties` to project only fields you care about.

list.usage_instructions: List advertiser programs from CJ's Advertiser Lookup REST API. Defaults to `--relationship joined` -- pass `--relationship notjoined` to find candidates to apply to. Combine with `--keywords` / `--category` / `--name` for server-side narrowing; the API caps `--limit` at 100 per page (use `--page` for paging).

options.name: --limit
options.help: Maximum number of records (CJ caps page size at 100)

options.name: --relationship
options.help: Relationship slice: joined | notjoined | all | <comma-separated advertiser IDs>

search.help: Wildcard search advertiser names. Server-side keyword search plus client-side fnmatch so explicit ``*`` wildcards work.
search.usage_instructions: Wildcard search advertiser names. Server-side keyword narrowing plus client-side fnmatch so explicit `*` wildcards work (`cj advertisers search '*hosting*'`). Use `--relationship` to restrict the search slice (default `all`).

options.name: --relationship
options.help: Relationship slice to search within (joined | notjoined | all)

auth.usage_instructions: Manage CJ credentials. `login` walks through the dual-auth flow (paste PAT, then complete interactive browser login). `status` returns the per-profile, per-credential-type JSON used by other CLIs. `profiles` supports multi-account switching.

login.help: Configure authentication credentials. Prompts for required credentials based on the tool's authentication type. For OAuth authorization code flows, opens a browser for user consent.
login.usage_instructions: Walk the user through configuring the CJ PAT and (when browser_session is required) the persistent Marketplace browser session. Use `--force` to redo login even when a session already exists.

options.name: --profile
options.help: Profile name to save credentials to

logout.help: Clear stored credentials and browser sessions.
logout.usage_instructions: Clear stored credentials and the persistent browser session for the active profile.

options.name: --profile
options.help: Profile name to clear credentials from

profiles.usage_instructions: Manage named auth profiles when you need to drive more than one CJ publisher account from this machine.

create.help: Create a new profile from .env.example template.
create.usage_instructions: Create a new named profile and walk through its auth flow.

delete.help: Delete a profile and its data.
delete.usage_instructions: Remove a profile and its stored credentials.

get.help: Get details for a specific profile.
get.usage_instructions: Show details for one profile (does not print credentials).

arguments.name: name
arguments.help: Profile name to get details for

list.help: List all profiles and show which is the default.

options.name: --limit
options.help: Maximum number of profiles to return

options.name: --properties
options.help: Comma-separated list of fields to include

remove.help: Delete a profile and its data.

select.help: Activate a profile within its auth type.

arguments.name: name
arguments.help: Profile name to activate within its auth type

status.help: Check authentication status across profiles. Performs a live round-trip for every configured credential type so the report reflects ground truth — not on-disk belief. Saved credentials whose live verification fails are reported as ``authenticated: false`` with the failure reason in ``api_test`` (API/OAuth) or via the live browser check (browser_session).
status.usage_instructions: Emit JSON describing every profile's credential state -- the per-credential `credentials_saved` / `authenticated` keys other tools rely on.

options.name: --profile
options.help: Profile name to check (defaults to all profiles)

test.help: Test authentication by verifying credentials work across profiles.
test.usage_instructions: Run the configured `test_connection()` to validate the PAT can hit advertiser-lookup. Exits non-zero on credential or network failure.

cache.usage_instructions: Manage the local HTTP response cache. Use `clear` to invalidate cached advertiser-lookup results when the publisher's relationship state changes out-of-band.

clear.help: Remove all cached responses. Deletes every file in the cache directory ({storage_dir}/cache/) and reports how many files and bytes were freed.
clear.usage_instructions: Empty the local response cache for cj.

links.help: Search creatives and generate affiliate tracking URLs
links.usage_instructions: Search advertiser creatives and generate affiliate deep-link tracking URLs. `list` and `get` hit CJ's Link Search REST API (PAT only, no browser); `deeplink` builds tracking URLs offline using `CJ_PUBLISHER_ACCOUNT_ID` from the profile env. Workflow: confirm a joined relationship via `cj relationships get`, list creatives with `cj links list <advertiser_id>`, then either copy a `click_url` from the listing or mint a custom destination URL with `cj links deeplink`.

deeplink.help: Generate a CJ deep-link tracking URL. The generated URL routes through CJ's redirector (``anrdoezrs.net``) and lands on the supplied destination URL on the advertiser's site.  Clicks are credited to the publisher identified by ``CJ_PUBLISHER_ACCOUNT_ID``. Requires ``CJ_PUBLISHER_ACCOUNT_ID`` in the profile env file -- this is the numeric segment between ``/member/`` and ``/publisher/`` in any authenticated members.cj.com URL.
examples: cj links deeplink 4837117 https://nordvpn.com/torrent --no-verify  # skip relationship check
deeplink.usage_instructions: Generate a CJ deep-link affiliate tracking URL offline (no API call) that routes a destination URL through CJ's redirector (`anrdoezrs.net`) and credits the publisher. Requires `CJ_PUBLISHER_ACCOUNT_ID` in the active profile's env file (the numeric segment between `/member/` and `/publisher/` in any authenticated members.cj.com URL). Use `--sid` to tag the link for CJ reports (e.g. blog-post slug or campaign id). `--verify` (default on) does a quick REST relationship check before emitting; pass `--no-verify` to skip it for batch generation when relationships are already confirmed.

arguments.name: destination_url
arguments.help: Destination URL on the advertiser site

options.name: --sid
options.help: Publisher sub-id (tracking tag for CJ reports, e.g. "blog-post-slug")

options.name: --verify
options.help: Confirm the publisher has a joined relationship with the advertiser before emitting

get.help: Get full detail for a single creative by link id. CJ's Link Search API has no per-id endpoint -- this iterates a page of results client-side.  For better performance when the advertiser is known, use ``cj links list <advertiser-id>`` and filter by ``link_id`` in the output.
get.usage_instructions: Get full detail for a single creative by link ID. CJ's Link Search API has no per-id endpoint, so this iterates results client-side -- when the advertiser ID is known, `cj links list <advertiser_id> --filter link_id:eq:<id>` is significantly faster.

list.help: List creatives for an advertiser via CJ's Link Search API. The publisher must have a joined relationship with the advertiser for CJ to return any links; otherwise the response is empty.
list.usage_instructions: List affiliate creatives (text links, banners, click links, deep-link templates, etc.) for one advertiser via CJ's Link Search REST API. Requires `advertiser_id` and a joined relationship with that advertiser (otherwise CJ returns an empty list -- use `cj relationships get <advertiser_id>` to pre-flight). Use `--type` to narrow to a creative type (`Text Link`, `Banner`, `Click Link`), `--promotion-type` for coupon/sale-specific creatives, `--keywords` for free-text search. REST-backed (PAT only); no browser session.

arguments.name: advertiser_id
arguments.help: CJ advertiser ID to list creatives for

options.name: --limit
options.help: Maximum number of records (CJ caps page size at 100)

options.name: --type
options.help: Filter by CJ link type (e.g. "Text Link", "Banner", "Click Link")

relationships.usage_instructions: Publisher-to-advertiser relationship management. `list` and `get` are REST-backed (PAT only). `apply` and `apply-bulk` drive the publisher Marketplace UI via the persistent browser session because CJ has no public mutation for joining a program; both are idempotent (they check existing relationship state first).

apply.help: Apply to (join) a single advertiser program. Idempotent: if the publisher is already joined / pending / declined, the command reports the existing status without re-submitting.
apply.usage_instructions: Submit a join application for one advertiser. Idempotent: when the REST API already reports `joined` / `pending` / `declined`, the command short-circuits without clicking. `--dry-run` reports what would happen without driving the browser. Failures capture a screenshot under the profile data directory.

arguments.name: advertiser_id
arguments.help: The CJ advertiser ID to apply to

options.name: --dry-run
options.help: Check existing relationship and report what would happen, but do not click Apply

options.name: --table
options.help: Display result as a table row

apply-bulk.help: Apply to many advertiser programs from a file or stdin. Each input line is one advertiser ID; comments (``#``) and blank lines are ignored.  Results stream as JSON (or a streaming table) so a downstream agent can act on each row as it lands.
examples: cj advertisers list --relationship notjoined --properties advertiser_id | jq -r '.[].advertiser_id'             | cj relationships apply-bulk -
apply-bulk.usage_instructions: Apply to many advertisers from a file (one ID per line) or stdin (`-`). Rate-limits between submissions via `--delay` (default 3s). Streams a JSON object per result so a downstream agent can react row-by-row; pass `--table` to buffer and print as a table instead. `--stop-on-error` halts on the first FAILED outcome.

arguments.name: source
arguments.help: Path to a file with one advertiser ID per line. Omit or pass '-' to read stdin

options.name: --delay
options.help: Seconds to sleep between applies (rate-limit). Default 3s

options.name: --dry-run
options.help: Report what would happen without clicking Apply

options.name: --stop-on-error
options.help: Halt on the first FAILED outcome instead of continuing

get.help: Get the current relationship for a single advertiser.
get.usage_instructions: Look up the current relationship for a single advertiser ID. Useful as a pre-flight before `apply`.

list.help: List the publisher's current advertiser relationships.
list.usage_instructions: List the publisher's current relationships filtered by `--status` (joined / notjoined / pending / declined / all). Read-only; uses the PAT only.

options.name: --status
options.help: Relationship status: joined | notjoined | pending | declined | all

usage_instructions: Use for CJ Affiliate publisher workflows -- discover advertiser programs in the CJ Marketplace, bulk-apply to them, and retrieve affiliate creatives or generate deep-link tracking URLs. The domain command groups are `advertisers` (read-only catalogue), `relationships` (the publisher's joined / pending / declined state plus the browser-driven apply flow), and `links` (creative search via the Link Search REST API plus offline deep-link URL generation). Auth requires BOTH a Personal Access Token (REST API, generated at https://members.cj.com/member/PersonalAccessTokens/tokens.cj) and a browser session to https://members.cj.com (the Marketplace join action has no public API). `cj links deeplink` additionally requires `CJ_PUBLISHER_ACCOUNT_ID` in the profile env. List commands honor --table, --limit, --filter, --properties; all output is JSON on stdout by default.
